title: [InstCombine] Replace all dominated uses of condition with constants

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i1 @select_in_dominated_block(i32 %x, i1 %ctrl) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %ctrl, label %taken, label %not_taken

taken:
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i1 true, i1 false
  ret i1 %sel

not_taken:
  ret i1 false
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i1 @select_in_dominated_block(i32 %x, i1 %ctrl) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %ctrl, label %taken, label %not_taken

taken:
  ret i1 true

not_taken:
  ret i1 false
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions comparisons, phi nodes, and calls but does not explicitly cover select instructions. The select instruction is a different user type that benefits from the generic dominance-based replacement.

taken:                                            ; preds = %entry

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @select_in_dominated_block(i32 %x, i1 %ctrl) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %ctrl, label %taken, label %not_taken

not_taken:
  ret i1 0

taken:
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i1 1, i1 0
  ret i1 %sel
}
=>
define i1 @select_in_dominated_block(i32 %x, i1 %ctrl) {
entry:
  br i1 %ctrl, label %taken, label %not_taken

not_taken:
  ret i1 0

taken:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i1 @condition_in_nested_dominated_chain(i32 %x, i1 %ctrl) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %ctrl, label %taken, label %not_taken

taken:
  br label %inner

inner:
  %use = zext i1 %cmp to i32
  %result = icmp eq i32 %use, 1
  ret i1 %result

not_taken:
  ret i1 false
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i1 @condition_in_nested_dominated_chain(i32 %x, i1 %ctrl) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, 0
  br i1 %ctrl, label %taken, label %not_taken

taken:
  br label %inner

inner:
  ret i1 true

not_taken:
  ret i1 false
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes immediate successor dominance but this test shows transitive dominance through an intermediate block (entry->taken->inner). The condition use in 'inner' is dominated by the true edge through a chain.

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i32 @select_dominated_condition(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, %y
  br i1 %cmp, label %true_br, label %false_br

true_br:
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i32 100, i32 200
  ret i32 %sel

false_br:
  %sel2 = select i1 %cmp, i32 300, i32 400
  ret i32 %sel2
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i32 @select_dominated_condition(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %x, %y
  br i1 %cmp, label %true_br, label %false_br

true_br:
  ret i32 100

false_br:
  ret i32 400
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions comparisons, phi nodes, and calls but does not explicitly cover select instructions. The condition is used as the selector operand in select instructions within dominated blocks, which is a different instruction pattern than the assume/call/compare patterns in existing tests.
